name: COMPANION VETERINARY CLINIC
address: 3197 N HWY 97 ST A1, BEND, OR 97701

fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Pedestrian Walkways; Sidewalk Network; Perimeter Fencing
fr.considerations: Single Access Point; Limited Rear Access
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access and operational efficiency could be achieved by refining the current property layout. Introducing an additional access point may facilitate improved entry and exit routes for responders, easing congestion and supporting quicker response times. Improving designated emergency staging areas near the main building, away from vehicle parking, would support efficient organization and resource management during operations. Enhancing pathways within the pedestrian walkways and sidewalk network to ensure they are unobstructed could improve responder movement and ease navigation around the property. Establishing clear signage near perimeter fencing outlining access routes and entry points could assist with swift location identification. Ensuring that pathways leading to all areas of the property, including the limited-rear access zones, remain clear and navigable at all times could further support emergency operations.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Limited Defensible Space
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space and reducing fuel continuity around the property could improve wildfire resilience. Increasing the separation between tree clusters and the structure may reduce structure exposure to radiant heat and flames. Managing vegetative growth adjacent to the structure by trimming back or removing excess plant material can help limit the potential for fire to spread to the building. Addressing scattered trees by pruning lower limbs and removing dead material will support diminishing vertical fuel continuity. Expanding defensible space by creating a buffer zone free of dense vegetation around the structure and promoting the maintenance of open space could further protect the property and support firefighter operations. Implementing these practical mitigation actions may strengthen long-term wildfire preparedness and promote the safety of the property.
